title: ADHD Trials Can't Tell Withdrawal from Relapse
description: The clinical trials that justify long-term stimulant use for ADHD contain a structural flaw: they cannot distinguish between a patient whose symptoms have returned and a patient whose brain is simply in withdrawal. This piece investigates how that design choice — common, industry-funded, and rarely disclosed to patients — has quietly shaped a generation of prescribing guidelines. It covers the neurobiological mechanism of stimulant dependence, the conflict-of-interest data in ADHD research, and what the long-term outcome evidence actually shows.
